zh.Fen Drayton, Cambridgeshire, England: 英國坎布利其爾芬 德雷頓
zh.Woodbridge, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可伍德布里奇
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice de Ufford於1310年出生在英國素福爾可帕哈姆時，她的父親Robert de Ufford II31歲，母親Cecily de Valoignes29歲。她於大約1333年與Thomas de Pabenham在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1370年享年60歲。

zh-hans.Fen Drayton, Cambridgeshire, England: 英国坎布利其爾芬 德雷頓
zh-hans.Woodbridge, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可伍德布里奇
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice de Ufford于1310年出生在英国素福爾可帕哈姆时，她的父亲Robert de Ufford II31岁，母亲Cecily de Valoignes29岁。她于大约1333年与Thomas de Pabenham在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1370年享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ufford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ufford (Northamptonshire), Ufford (Suffolk), or Offord Cluny or Offord Darcy (Huntingdonshire). The Northamptonshire and Suffolk placenames derive from the Old English personal name Uffa + Old English worth ‘enclosure’. For the Huntingdonshire placename see Offord , with which the surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Offord
meanings: English (East Anglia and London): habitational name from Offord Cluny and Offord Darcy in Huntindgonshire. The placename derives from Old English upp ‘up, upper’ + ford ‘ford’.
English: alternatively, from a lost place called Offord near Pennyford in Wootton Wawen, Warwickshire. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Offa + Old English worth ‘enclosure’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice de Ufford was born in 1310, in Parham, Suffolk, England, her father, Robert de Ufford II, was 31 and her mother, Cecily de Valoignes, was 29. She married Thomas de Pabenham about 1333, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1370, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice de Ufford est née en 1310, à Parham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Ufford II, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Valoignes, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas de Pabenham environ 1333, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1370, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice de Ufford 1310 in Parham, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Ufford II 31 und ihre Mutter Cecily de Valoignes 29. Sie hat um 1333 in England, United Kingdom Thomas de Pabenham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1370 im Alter von 60 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice de Ufford è nata il nel 1310 nel Parham, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Robert de Ufford II, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Cecily de Valoignes, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Thomas de Pabenham circa 1333, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1370, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice de Uffordが1310年にイギリススフォルクパーハムで生まれた時，父Robert de Ufford IIは31歳，母Cecily de Valoignesは29歳であった。約1333年にイギリスイギリスでThomas de Pabenhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1370年に60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Ufford(이)가 1310년 영국 수폴크 파람에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Ufford II(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Valoignes(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 대략 1333년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Thomas de Pabenham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1370년 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice de Ufford nasceu em 1310, em Parham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Ufford II, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Cecily de Valoignes, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas de Pabenham aproximadamente 1333, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1370, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice de Ufford родилась в 1310 г. в местности парам, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Ufford II было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Valoignes, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Pabenham около 1333 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1370 г. в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice de Ufford nació en 1310, en Parham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Ufford II, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Cecily de Valoignes, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Thomas de Pabenham aproximadamente 1333, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1370, a la edad de 60 años.
